title: Initial quadlet version integrated in golang
body:
Based on the initial port in https://github.com/containers/quadlet/pull/41

This should pass make lint, and has some initial tests (ported from the C unit tests).
Remaining work:
 * Convert the quadlet intergration tests
 * Add compilte time options (tags) for quadlet username, and fallback uid/gids
 * Actually install the generator in the right places
 * Documents the code a bit more
 * Actually test this live in the system

```release-note
Added new systemd-generator that can be used to easily write and maintain systemd services using podman (based on quadlet project).
```

comments.body:
I installed via  `sudo cp bin/quadlet /usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/quadlet-system-generator` (after removing traditional quadlet), and my quadlets services seemed to work on reboot:
```
$ systemctl status quadlet-minimal
● quadlet-minimal.service - A minimal container
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/containers/systemd/quadlet-minimal.container; generated)
     Active: active (running) since Mon 2022-10-03 16:48:00 CEST; 16s ago
    Process: 7688 ExecStartPre=rm -f /run/quadlet-minimal.cid (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
   Main PID: 7824 (conmon)
      Tasks: 3 (limit: 38377)
     Memory: 1.2M
        CPU: 271ms
     CGroup: /system.slice/quadlet-minimal.service
             ├─libpod-payload-b230b21be106d9337cf78e919368e4b714b0431a922177580af334917e519beb
             │ ├─ 7827 /run/podman-init -- sleep 60
             │ └─ 7872 sleep 60
             └─runtime
               └─ 7824 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c b230b21be106d9337cf78e919368e4b714b0431a922177580af334917e519beb -u b230b21be106d9337cf78e919368e4b714b0431a922177580af334917e519beb -r /usr/bin/crun -b /var/lib/containers/storag>
```
I looked at the time spent in generators in the `systemd-analyze plot` output, and there seems to be no real difference.
New version, now with the quadlet testcases imported to the e2e test

Now with some more comments, config options and we install binaries in the right places.

This is probably a good starting point for initial merge.
Just returned from PTO.  I will have a look at this PR this week.  Currently, CI is red.
Just a rebase on master to see if the vendoring issue still happens in ci

Once this is rebase/squashed then we can merge.
LGTM
